_documentation.description: Reference template for a per-form response-processing flow. Current auto-created flows use this same raw_response passthrough pattern; this file is for manual recreation, troubleshooting, or environments where you do not want the registration flow to create the data flow for you. This is NOT an importable flow definition.
notes: The preferred path is self-service registration, which creates these per-form flows automatically.
This manual template mirrors the generated flow structure: Get response details, POST form_id + raw_response to /api/process-response, and send an Outlook alert on failure.
Auto-created flows embed the current FUNCTION_APP_KEY value when they are generated. If you build this flow manually, keep the key in sync after rotation.
If you want runtime key lookup instead of an embedded key, use power-automate/flow-template-keyvault.json.
All PHI handling and de-identification remains server-side in the Azure Function.

trigger.type: When a new response is submitted
trigger._comment_trigger: This trigger fires each time a respondent submits the configured Microsoft Form.
trigger._comment_setup: When configuring the flow, select the target clinician form from the Form Id dropdown.

actions.name: Get_response_details
actions._comment: Retrieves the full Forms payload so the HTTP step can forward it unchanged as raw_response.

actions.name: Call_Azure_Function
actions._comment: POST the full Forms response to the Azure Function. This matches the current generated-flow body shape.

actions.name: Check_response_status
actions._comment: If the Azure Function call fails or times out, send an admin alert. Otherwise no additional step is required.

if_true._comment: Success. The Azure Function has already validated the form status, handled de-identification, and written to OneLake/Fabric.

inputs.subject: Forms to Fabric - Pipeline error: response @{triggerOutputs()?['body/responseId']}
inputs.body:
Forms to Fabric pipeline error

A form response failed to process.

Form ID: @{triggerOutputs()?['body/formId']}

Response ID: @{triggerOutputs()?['body/responseId']}

HTTP Status Code: @{outputs('Call_Azure_Function')?['statusCode']}

Error Details:

@{body('Call_Azure_Function')}

Timestamp: @{utcNow()}

If the response mentions unavailable Fabric capacity, resume the capacity and retry the flow.

AZURE_FUNCTION_URL.description: Full URL of the Azure Function endpoint, including /api/process-response (for example: https://<function-app-name>.azurewebsites.net/api/process-response).

AZURE_FUNCTION_KEY.description: Current host or function key used by the HTTP action. Keep it in sync with FUNCTION_APP_KEY if you also use generated flows.

ADMIN_NOTIFICATION_EMAIL.description: Monitored mailbox or distribution list that receives failure alerts.

FORM_ID.description: Microsoft Form ID for the clinician data-collection form.

setup_instructions: 1. Prefer the self-service registration flow if you want per-form flows created automatically.
2. If you need a manual flow, create a new Automated Cloud Flow in Power Automate.
3. Select the 'When a new response is submitted' trigger and choose the target form.
4. Add 'Get response details' and keep the action name as Get_response_details if you want to copy the expressions exactly.
5. Add an HTTP action that POSTs to <function-app>/api/process-response with Content-Type and x-functions-key headers.
6. Use the body shape shown in this template: form_id plus raw_response from body('Get_response_details').
7. Add a condition that checks for status code 200, and on failure send an Outlook email to the admin mailbox.
8. Save the flow and test with a sample submission.
